title: Archaic Gathering Festival
body: Australian are really good at creating a space to party, colorful and playful, with the most high tech laser, sound systems, and amazing artists for decorating and visuals. This festival was one of the best decorated one I've been to. Sadly, the music wasn't too good for us, a lot of drum&bass and dubstep which is a style I don't like. The Saturday was really nice, with a lot of deeper stuff like pay trance in the night.

title: Eungella National Park
body: After thinking about this moment for so long, we finally found the platypus! A beautiful sunrise over the broken river, and a couple hours waiting with the binoculars, from the first lights, and we managed to sight the creature feeding, swimming and just going around. It was a very special experience and one that I'll remember forever.

title: Cape Hillsborough
body:
After sleep at the beautiful Halliday Bay, we took a sunrise walk on the beach at cape Hillsborough, and a nice hike up the rock, what a stunning view! Loved it so much. 
Later we did the board walk, and almost drove on a massive python. 🐍

title: Agnès water & 1770
body: Agnes water and 1770 were one of the highlights of this east coast travels.
Good surf! A beautiful right waves, point break. Few people, and some punch to it, even barrels. Bori took her first real waves here, and started to enjoy it.
Good fishing, lots of people on boats. We mostly got a few small ones from the jetty, but still fun.
On hiccup: I did an oil change on a parking, cross threaded the filter, got us stranded for a day, but a random local helped us and we were really lucky to have met him, he got us back on the road.
I really loved our time in here, wish we could stay longer.

title: Rainbow Beach
body:
From the ferry after Noosa, all the way to Rainbow beach, we were off-road in pristine driving conditions. We were right in time to make it out with the tide and through the tracks taking us up the dunes and into rainbow beach area. 
Fuji behaved so well, no issues, no overheating. (Thanks the new radiator!)

title: Jervis Bay
body:
A shorter trip to Jervis, over a long weekend. This place is just like a postal card, most beaches are empty.
Jervis bay is a territory of its own, stealth camping isn't permitted, but we always find a way around that.

title: Blue Mountains
body: The blue mountains is a special place with gorgeous hikes. When the sky is clear you can see the three sisters from Katoomba, quite a sight, with the cockatoo flying over the cliff edges.

title: Hat Head
body:
The headlands are beautiful, but hat head was next level. Coming north from Crescent Head, we found our way through some crazy 4x4 paths, allowing us to test Fuji at a higher potential. 
We found a breathtaking landscape, sunset over the cliffs, and a whole crowd of kangoroos just chilling around, not minding us being close to them, fascinated by their presence.

title: Worimi Regional Park
summary: Fuji's first time on the beach
body:
Beautiful beach, with a huge sand bar and a forest behind. 
Fuji was overheating like crazy on the sand, we already knew it might happen. I'll get a new oversized radiator in the next town, so it won't happen again.

title: Rabbits Eat Lettuce Festival
summary: A beautiful place full of crazy people
body: While this is one of the most commercial doofs in Australia, it also has an amazing vibe to offer. We had a great time at Rabbits and got to experience a proper Australian dancfloor.

title: Byron Hinterland
body: While the famous town of Byron wasn't too impressive for us, the rest of the region was magical. The people we met & the experiences we had will stay in my heart forever.

title: Sydney - The Nothern Beaches
body: The Nothern Beaches, Freshwater, was our home for the first few months in Australia. We had a great experience, discovering the region while working and saving to get our car, Fuji.
